shared_mechanism: universal eigenvalue-spacing statistics with level repulsion (GUE sine-kernel pair-correlation / Montgomery-Odlyzko law) — gap probability vanishing linearly, independent of microscopic detail
brief_reason: The normalized spacings between consecutive nontrivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function on the critical line follow the same random-matrix level-repulsion law (Montgomery's pair-correlation conjecture, verified numerically by Odlyzko), so zeta zeros behave statistically like the eigenvalues of a large random Hermitian ensemble.
